baseline.note: ledger totals that predate canonical lifecycle accounting (accounted explicitly)

reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

tradingview_latest_signal.indicator_name: Hermes BTC Pulse Composite v4 Orderflow Loose

BTCUSD.indicator_name: Hermes BTC Pulse Composite v4 Orderflow Loose

tradingview.note: TradingView alerts are candidate signals only — they cannot place, resize, or bypass trades; the strategy + execution gate remain the sole trade authority.

edge_vs_5min_outcome.note: observe-only: did the TradingView signal at entry predict the 5-min Chainlink outcome (signal_hit_rate vs baseline_up_rate), and did aligning help the bot win (aligned vs opposed bot_win_rate)? Never affects trading.

rsi_trend.note: observe-only: learns P(next 5-min outcome | RSI alert trend state) from the alert history and scores its own next-window predictions. Never trades.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

signal_gate.note: when active, a paper trade is taken only if a fresh TradingView signal agrees with the side; it can only PREVENT trades, never force or bypass them.

context_gate.note: hard prior gate: blocks proven-losing entry contexts (volume spikes, noise regime, far-from-resolution) before enough samples exist for the learned selectivity gate. Can only PREVENT trades — never forces, sizes, or bypasses the execution gate, which remains the sole trade authority.

learning.note: the bot's own settled-trade experience (calibrated edge model) adjusts the directional probability; it grows as more trades settle. The execution-quality gate, paper-realism, and reconciliation are unchanged and still veto every trade — learning can never bypass them.

last_note.summary: DOWN_STRONG (n=62, wr=0.597) and range_bottom (n=32, wr=0.781) show confirmed positive EV with n>=8 and wr above breakeven after costs; overall sample remains small (107 trades) with net negative realized pnl driven by UP and short-ttc buckets. Most other slices are either n<8 or show mixed/noisy results (e.g., volume_spike, zscore 1..2).
warnings: Total realized pnl still negative (-24.6) despite positive avg_ev in most buckets; small n and possible selection bias in accepted trades only.
focus_next: Accumulate samples in range_bottom + DOWN_STRONG intersection
Track ttc>=120s and dead-volume slices for stability
Monitor whether UP_weak and mixed mtf remain unprofitable as n grows

history.summary: With 93 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals (n=51, WR 60.8%) and range_bottom entries (n=27, WR 74.1%) show confirmed positive EV after costs with sufficient samples; DOWN overall outperforms UP materially while short-hold (<60s) and volume-spike buckets remain clearly negative. Most other buckets are still too small or mixed to confirm. Sample remains modest so selectivity on DOWN + favorable regime combinations is the only emerging edge.

DOWN_STRONG/DOWN_WEAK signals in trending regimes with ttc>=120s, range_bottom, lower_wick_rejection, bearish_aligned, dead/active volume, and v4 indicator show win-rates 0.60-0.75 above breakeven with positive EV after costs and n>=20; these are confirmed. UP signals, volume_spike, range_top, bull_close_near_high, and most z-score extremes remain unprofitable or noisy with negative realized pnl despite positive avg_ev. Overall sample of 95 is still small and selective rejection rate extremely high.

With 97 settled trades, DOWN signals (esp. DOWN_STRONG) and a handful of regime buckets show modestly positive realized PnL and win-rates above 0.55-0.60, while UP and most other slices remain negative or too small; avg_ev_after_cost stays positive across buckets but has not yet translated into consistent net profitability. No buckets meet strict confirmation criteria of confidently beating their own breakeven win-rate with material positive EV and n>>50. Sample remains too limited and noisy for live deployment.
changes_since_last: No prior analysis provided; this is baseline view

history.summary: DOWN_STRONG, range_bottom, lower_wick_rejection, dead volume, and bearish_aligned buckets now show confirmed positive EV with n>=13, win-rates 0.57-0.77 above breakeven, and net positive realized pnl after costs; most other regimes remain noise with negative aggregate pnl of -33.88 USD across 100 trades. UP direction and short ttc (<60s) buckets are clear underperformers. No prior analysis provided so no changes tracked yet.

With 101 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG/DOWN_WEAK signals (n=56/21) and range_bottom/lower_wick_rejection states show confirmed edge via win-rates 0.57-0.77 above breakeven, positive realized PnL, and n>=8; longer time-to-close (>=120s) and dead-volume regimes also hold after costs. Most other buckets (z-score, Hurst, spread, HTF bias) remain near-breakeven or negative with no reliable separation. Overall book is still slightly negative despite uniformly positive avg_EV, indicating selectivity or sizing issues.

DOWN_STRONG signals (n=58, WR=0.586) and select sub-buckets (range_bottom n=29 WR=0.759, lower_wick_rejection n=13 WR=0.769, dead volume n=44 WR=0.591, bearish_aligned n=40 WR=0.575, unknown cvd n=52 WR=0.596) show WR above plausible breakeven with positive realized PnL and n>=8; all other buckets remain marginal or negative after costs despite positive avg EV. UP direction and short-hold (<60s) buckets are clear underperformers. Sample is still small and regime-skewed toward trending/bearish conditions.

DOWN_STRONG and range_bottom entries show confirmed edge with n>=13, win rates 0.59-0.77 above breakeven, positive realized pnl and stable positive EV after costs; most other buckets remain noise given mixed/negative pnl despite uniformly positive EV estimates. UP direction and volume_spike conditions are clearly failing with negative pnl and sub-breakeven results. Overall sample still tiny (106 trades) and net pnl negative, so patterns are early-stage only.

DOWN_STRONG (n=62, wr=0.597) and range_bottom (n=32, wr=0.781) show confirmed positive EV with n>=8 and wr above breakeven after costs; overall sample remains small (107 trades) with net negative realized pnl driven by UP and short-ttc buckets. Most other slices are either n<8 or show mixed/noisy results (e.g., volume_spike, zscore 1..2).

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only — never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only — never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

aggression.note: loosens (more explore/looser exploit/larger size) as acted trades profit; tightens on losses; circuit breaker is the hard floor.

grok_decider.note: Grok decides; bot executes (paper). shadow=decide+grade only (no trade), follow=engine follows direction/size subject to the deterministic floor (execution realism, risk caps, freshness). Fail-closed -> no_trade.

headlines: Ongoing US spot BTC ETF outflows (recent sessions -$68M to -$90M; $6.35B over 30 days)
Macro storm: Fed hawkish, BOJ rate hike signals, risk assets dumping (BTC -3.5%)
BTC consolidating below $63K with bearish tilt; PCE inflation watch

verifier.note: independent Claude maker-checker; can only veto/shrink a Grok-proposed paper trade, never force one; fail-open; PAPER ONLY.

last_note.summary: The bot shows no statistically significant edge across 363 settled trades: 52.6% win rate, -$85 PnL, profit factor 0.84, and calibration error (Brier 0.23) near random baseline (0.25). Most contexts are losing or coin-flip. The only positive signals are small-sample anomalies (zscore -2..-1: n=4, 75% WR; noise regime: n=1, 100% WR) that lack statistical power and likely reflect noise rather than edge.

knob_recommendations.reason: Current avg EV after costs (0.106) is not translating to profit; raise threshold to filter marginal trades
Conviction <0.2 bucket lost -$16 at 30% WR; eliminate weakest signals
With no proven edge, dramatically reduce trade frequency to conserve capital and focus on highest-conviction outliers only

new_lessons.rule: 5-minute BTC direction prediction at 52.6% WR over 363 samples confirms near-market efficiency; do not assume edge exists without >>55% WR and positive PnL over n≥200
Ignore any bucket with n<20: zscore -2..-1 (n=4, 75% WR) and noise regime (n=1, 100% WR) are statistically meaningless and likely regression artifacts
Execution gate rejected 42 negative-EV-after-slippage candidates; this is protective but insufficient when base model has no edge
Brier score 0.23 vs baseline 0.25 is only marginally better than random; calibration alone does not imply profitability in directional betting
Avg loss $3.94 > avg win $2.99 at 52.6% WR mathematically guarantees losses; must achieve ≥57% WR to break even at this payoff ratio

research_loop.note: strategy-layer research meta-loop: independent model proposes exploit/avoid contexts + bounded knob nudges + lessons. Observe-only unless auto_apply (whitelisted+bounded). PAPER ONLY.

recent.rule: execution gate rejected 42 trades as negative_ev_after_slippage; gate is working but pre-gate model still unprofitable
ttc <120s buckets lose more (33-40% WR) vs 120-240s (51.9% WR); sub-2min window adds execution noise, no edge
stale_polymarket_down n=14 71%WR +$12 is noise; no mechanism for stale orderbook → direction edge
zscore -2..-1 n=4 positive is too small; do not chase mean-reversion without n>=30 and stable profit factor
trending (n=36) and noise (n=1) regimes both near breakeven; Hurst not predictive of edge in 5-min BTC
conviction <0.2 lost -$24 on n=9 22%WR; model uncertainty is informative—respect it
edge_quality:high n=34 50%WR -$21;LabVIEW feature scoring does not predict realized edge
360 trades, all major slices losing or coin-flip; no context shows repeatable alpha above execution costs
5-minute BTC direction prediction at 52.6% WR over 363 samples confirms near-market efficiency; do not assume edge exists without >>55% WR and positive PnL over n≥200
Ignore any bucket with n<20: zscore -2..-1 (n=4, 75% WR) and noise regime (n=1, 100% WR) are statistically meaningless and likely regression artifacts
Brier score 0.23 vs baseline 0.25 is only marginally better than random; calibration alone does not imply profitability in directional betting
Avg loss $3.94 > avg win $2.99 at 52.6% WR mathematically guarantees losses; must achieve ≥57% WR to break even at this payoff ratio

loops.note: formalized sub-loops (data/signal/verify/execute/risk/research/news/lessons); each has a trigger + skill + verifier + verifiable stop. PAPER ONLY.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

cex_lead_edge.promotion_rule: n>=min AND wilson_lower(win_rate)>breakeven AND Brier_cex<Brier_market AND avg_pnl>0
cex_lead_edge.note: CEX-lead latency edge; benchmark is the MARKET price (already ~0.21 Brier). SHADOW grades only; GATED can PROPOSE a side on a proven bucket but the execution-quality gate + safety floor remain authoritative. PAPER ONLY.

learned_selectivity_gate.note: rejects ONLY buckets confidently below their own breakeven win-rate (Wilson upper bound < breakeven AND net-negative PnL); coin-flip / not-significant buckets are NOT rejected. Can only make the bot MORE selective — never trades, resizes, or bypasses the execution gate. Exploration tracked separately.

counterfactual.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

gate.note: when enabled, only late-window AND high-conviction setups may trade; can only PREVENT trades, never force/size/bypass the execution gate.

edge_measurement.note: observe-only: grades whether late-window high-conviction trades win more (cohort vs other) from live settled trades; never affects trading.
